stem: An engineer takes 10 measurements of tire pressure using a pressure gauge. The measurements have a mean of 115.1 psi and a standard deviation of 16.0 psi. However, calibration tests reveal that a pressure gauge systematically underestimates the true value by between 4.9% and 10.2%. Estimate the true tire pressure and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 115.1 psi. After correcting for the 7.5% bias: ≈ 124.4 psi. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (4.9%-10.2%) and sampling variability: [110.9, 138.3]. Point estimate: 124.4 psi.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
